commits.messageBody:
…s v1 mode agents

Legacy Harness centrally wired memory, workspace, browser, and pubsub to every
static mode agent that did not own its own. The v1 runtime only propagated
workspace and browser, so custom modes whose agents did not own memory or
pubsub silently lost observation persistence and signal routing.

This restores the missing leg of the legacy contract:

  - Adds pubsub to MastraCodeRuntimeConfig.
  - createMastraCode now passes signalsPubSub into the runtime.
  - MastraCodeHarnessRuntime gains propagateRuntimeServicesToAgents, called
    once in the constructor over the v1 agent map with a Set<Agent> dedupe
    and hasOwnMemory / hasOwnPubSub guards.

Subagents and the default code-agent already inherit these services via
pre-injection in createMastraCode; the runtime guard makes the runtime pass
idempotent for those instances while finally covering custom mode agents.

Browser propagation and dynamic mode-agent re-resolution are intentionally
out of scope; the former lives in the browser-ownership rework, the latter
is a separate v1 compatibility concern.

Adds nine tests in mastracode/src/harness/runtime.test.ts covering memory +
pubsub propagation, owned-service preservation, dedupe across reused mode
agents and across config.agents + config.modes, dynamic-mode skip, and the
no-op when neither service is configured.
commits.messageHeadline: fix(mastracode): propagate runtime memory and pubsub to custom Harnes…

title: fix(mastracode): propagate runtime memory and pubsub to custom Harness v1 mode agents
